original_hypothesis.title: uint128 Overflow DoS Permanently Bricks Market via First-Depositor Attack
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In update_market, accCantoPerShare and secRewardsPerShare are stored as uint128. The secRewardsPerShare accumulation formula (blockDelta * 1e36 / marketSupply) causes a uint128 overflow revert when marketSupply is very small. An attacker can deposit 1 wei as the first depositor in a new market, and after ~340 blocks the uint128 cast overflows, making update_market permanently revert. Since sync_ledger calls update_market, no user can deposit, withdraw, or claim for that market ever again.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: secRewardsPerShare += uint128((blockDelta * 1e36) / marketSupply) always accumulates regardless of cantoPerBlock. With marketSupply = 1 wei, a single block adds 1e36 to secRewardsPerShare. After ~340 blocks, the accumulated value exceeds uint128 max (~3.4e38). Solidity 0.8 uint128() cast reverts on overflow.

proof_of_signal.pattern: market.secRewardsPerShare += uint128((blockDelta * 1e36) / marketSupply); with marketSupply = 1, single block yields 1e36, overflow uint128 max ~3.4e38 after ~340 blocks

preconditions: Newly whitelisted market with no existing deposits
Attacker can be the first depositor (via LiquidityGauge.depositUnderlying or direct sync_ledger)
~340 blocks pass (~30 minutes at 5.7s/block) before another large deposit

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: uint128((blockDelta * 1e36) / 1) = 1e36 per block; uint128 max ≈ 3.4e38; overflow at ~340 blocks
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Deposit 1 wei of underlying token via gauge, wait ~340 blocks
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing attack: costs 1 wei to permanently brick a market. All future deposits in that market fail.

economic_questions: Is there a minimum deposit enforced by the underlying token or lending market?
Can accCantoPerShare also overflow independently for high cantoPerBlock?
Can governance recover by re-whitelisting or resetting state?
validation_requirements: Calculate exact block count to overflow for 1 wei deposit
Test that update_market reverts after overflow block count
Verify all dependent functions (sync_ledger, claim, withdrawUnderlying) also fail

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas for one depositUnderlying tx (~100K gas)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Griefing attack; no direct profit but denies all market rewards
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: ~30 minutes after deposit for permanent effect

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after market is whitelisted, before legitimate deposits
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Permanent DoS of the market; all user funds in LiquidityGauge become stuck

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All future and current users of the bricked market
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of deposited underlying tokens stuck in gauge forever
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All TVL in the target market

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Exact overflow threshold, interaction with cantoPerBlock overflow
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Deploy and test with 1 wei deposit, verify revert after N blocks

validation_result.mechanism: uint128 overflow revert DoS via small deposit
validation_result.impact_summary: A first-depositor attack with 1 wei permanently bricks a market after ~340 blocks, as uint128 cast on secRewardsPerShare accumulation reverts in Solidity 0.8, blocking all deposits, withdrawals, and claims.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies that `secRewardsPerShare` (stored as uint128) accumulates via `uint128((blockDelta * 1e36) / marketSupply)` on every `update_market` call. When `marketSupply = 1` (1 wei deposit), each block adds `1e36` to the intermediate value. After ~340 blocks, the intermediate value (`blockDelta * 1e36`) exceeds `type(uint128).max ≈ 3.4e38`. In Solidity 0.8.17 (the configured compiler version), explicit narrowing casts like `uint128(x)` REVERT when `x > type(uint128).max` — they do not silently truncate. This revert propagates through `update_market` → `sync_ledger` → `_afterTokenTransfer`, making all LiquidityGauge operations (deposit, withdraw, transfer) permanently fail. Note: the Phase A security analysis incorrectly categorized this as 'silent truncation' at LOW severity; the actual behavior is a checked revert causing permanent DoS at CRITICAL severity.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 94-95 of LendingLedger.sol contain the vulnerable accumulation. The uint128 cast at line 94 will revert when `(blockDelta * 1e36) / marketSupply` exceeds `type(uint128).max`. With `marketSupply = 1`, this threshold is reached at `blockDelta > 340`. Even if iterations are split across epoch boundaries, the `+=` operator on the uint128 `secRewardsPerShare` field will also revert via Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic once the cumulative value exceeds uint128 max.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When marketSupply = 1 wei, the expression (blockDelta * 1e36) / 1 = blockDelta * 1e36. After 341 blocks, this equals 3.41e38 which exceeds type(uint128).max ≈ 3.40e38. In Solidity 0.8.17, uint128(3.41e38) reverts. Even with smaller blockDeltas per iteration, the += on uint128 secRewardsPerShare will overflow via checked arithmetic once the cumulative value exceeds uint128 max. This permanently bricks the market.

Every mint, burn, and transfer triggers sync_ledger, which calls update_market. Once update_market permanently reverts due to uint128 overflow, all token operations are blocked — including withdrawals of underlying tokens.

sync_ledger unconditionally calls update_market. When update_market reverts due to the uint128 overflow, sync_ledger also reverts, blocking all deposit and withdrawal operations through the LiquidityGauge.

calling_sequence: 1. Attacker calls gauge.depositUnderlying(1) — deposits 1 wei of underlying
4. sync_ledger resolves lendingMarket via liquidityGauges mapping
5. update_market is called — marketSupply is 0 so reward loop is skipped, lastRewardBlock set to block.number
7. ~341 blocks pass with no further interaction
8. Any call to update_market: marketSupply = 1, blockDelta >= 341
10. All subsequent sync_ledger calls revert, bricking deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and claims
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The attack path starts with depositUnderlying(1) at LiquidityGauge.sol:33, which calls _mint → _afterTokenTransfer (LiquidityGauge.sol:46) → sync_ledger (LendingLedger.sol:103) → update_market (LendingLedger.sol:73). On the initial deposit, update_market succeeds because marketSupply is 0. After sync_ledger sets lendingMarketTotalBalance to 1, the next update_market call (after 341+ blocks) hits the overflow at line 94: `market.secRewardsPerShare += uint128((blockDelta * 1e36) / marketSupply)` where blockDelta * 1e36 / 1 exceeds uint128 max. The Solidity 0.8 checked cast reverts, permanently bricking the market.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deploy.sol confirms Solidity 0.8 compilation. foundry.toml confirms solc_version = 0.8.17 with optimizer enabled. No minimum deposit amounts or market supply floors are configured.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack is fully validated: (1) Attacker deposits 1 wei via depositUnderlying — no minimum deposit check exists (LiquidityGauge.sol:33-37). (2) lendingMarketTotalBalance becomes 1 (LendingLedger.sol:119). (3) After 341 blocks (~32 minutes at 5.7s avg block time), update_market's line 94 computes uint128(341 * 1e36 / 1) = uint128(3.41e38) which exceeds type(uint128).max and reverts. (4) Since sync_ledger (called by all gauge operations) invokes update_market, all deposits, withdrawals, and claims permanently fail. (5) User funds deposited in the gauge after the attacker (if any deposited within the 341-block window) become permanently stuck. (6) Governance cannot fix this by un-whitelisting — update_market would still revert at the whitelist check, and re-whitelisting wouldn't reset secRewardsPerShare or lastRewardBlock to bypass the overflow window.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL: A 1-wei deposit permanently bricks a whitelisted market after ~32 minutes. All user funds deposited in the corresponding LiquidityGauge become irrecoverable. The attack costs negligible gas and 1 wei of the underlying token. Governance has no recovery mechanism since the overflow condition persists regardless of whitelist state changes. This affects every newly whitelisted market and can be systematically applied to grief the entire protocol.
recommended_mitigations: Use uint256 instead of uint128 for accCantoPerShare and secRewardsPerShare to eliminate overflow risk
Enforce a minimum deposit amount (e.g., 1e6 or 1e12) in depositUnderlying and sync_ledger to prevent extremely small marketSupply values
Add a minimum lendingMarketTotalBalance threshold in update_market, skipping reward accumulation when supply is below a safe floor
Remove the secRewardsPerShare accumulation entirely if secondary rewards are not used, eliminating the always-active overflow vector
Consider using unchecked blocks for the uint128 cast if truncation is acceptable, though this only masks the overflow without fixing the accounting error
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy all contracts via Deploy.sol. 2. Governance adds a gauge type and gauge via GaugeController. 3. Governance whitelists a market with gauge via whiteListLendingMarket. 4. Attacker approves gauge for 1 wei of underlying token. 5. Attacker calls depositUnderlying(1). 6. Advance 341 blocks using vm.roll(block.number + 341). 7. Call update_market(market) and verify it reverts with arithmetic overflow. 8. Attempt depositUnderlying(1e18) from another user and verify it also reverts. 9. Attempt withdrawUnderlying(1) from the attacker and verify it also reverts.
uncertainties: Whether Canto L1 block times could be significantly faster than 5.7s, which would shorten the attack window
Whether governance could deploy entirely new contracts to migrate around a bricked market (out-of-scope workaround)
Whether some underlying tokens have minimum transfer amounts that would prevent 1 wei deposits
additional_testing_needed: Forge test reproducing the exact overflow threshold and verifying revert
Test with different blockDelta values across epoch boundaries to confirm overflow in split-iteration scenarios
Test accCantoPerShare overflow independently when cantoPerBlock is set to large values
Test governance un-whitelist and re-whitelist flow to confirm no recovery path exists
